title: eriogonum batemanii var. eremicum
intro: Eriogonum batemanii var. eremicum, commonly known as desert buckwheat, is a perennial shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum batemanii var. eremicum include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and providing relief from digestive issues. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or salves, and are often used to treat various health conditions, including eczema, acne, and stomach ulcers.
